description: Every connection REFERENCE the M1 flow definitions bind to. A flow's host.connectionName resolves to one of these logical references; the reference binds to a real connection at deploy/activation by the user. NO live connection instances live here - binding a connection to a live Collision Engineers mailbox / Box account / SharePoint job sheet is [RESERVED-FOR-USER]. This is the closed set the linter checks every definition's connectionName against.

connectionReferences.note: Intake trigger. The live mailbox is bound by the user at activation; the mailbox address is a flow parameter (IntakeMailbox), never hardcoded. [RESERVED-FOR-USER] to point at a live inbox.

Working store + env-var reads. Flows READ environment variables (environmentvariabledefinitions); they never write environmentvariablevalue/definition. case-disposition (Phase 9) READS the cr1bd_CASE_DISPOSITION_ENABLED gate + the two-clock retention columns and anonymises Case/Evidence PII (field-null) + audits case_disposed; it never deletes cr1bd_auditevent rows.

Attachment/.eml bytes go here (never inline in a Dataverse row). The storage Function returns { Path, sha256, size }. finalize-eva-box also READS blob content here (Get blob content using path (V2), operationId GetFileContentByPath_V2, dataset=AccountNameFromSettings) to fetch real photo bytes before each Box CreateFile (the S2 content-bind fix) — account cespkevidstdev01, container evidence, access-key auth. box-blob-purge + case-disposition (Phase 9) DELETE transient Blob bytes here (GetFileMetadataByPath_V2 to resolve the opaque id, then DeleteFile_V2) — transient bytes only; the Box archive copy is NEVER touched. [RESERVED-FOR-USER] to bind.

KEPT for finalize-eva-box's BYTE path ONLY (the Phase-7 PIN per build-plan 04: parallel REST connector, first-party retained for bytes — NOT repointed in place). The Microsoft first-party Box connector is class STANDARD (verified Microsoft Learn connectors/box 2026-06-20) and has NO CreateFolder op — bytes upload via CreateFile after GetFileContentByPath_V2 (the S2 real-bytes fix). The UPPERCASE Case/PO FOLDER is no longer created here: it is minted at parse-confirm via the custom cr1bd_box_rest CreateFolder op (folder pre-exists, finalize augments). Interactive Box OAuth only (no JWT/CCG/SSO). BoxArchiveRootId migrates to read cr1bd_BOX_FOLDER_ROOT_ID. [RESERVED-FOR-USER] to activate (sign into the live Box account).

Phase-7 PIN (ADR-0012, build-plan 04 — supersedes the 'one unpinned decision'): a PARALLEL custom connector, NOT an in-place repoint of cr1bd_box. Fronts the box-webhook Azure Function (functions/box-webhook/). Service identity = Box Service Account (CCG); the Box CCG token is minted INSIDE the Function from a Key Vault client_secret (custom connectors CANNOT run client_credentials — Microsoft Learn). Auth on THIS connection = the Function host key (x-functions-key); the Box client_secret is a Function-side KV ref, NEVER here, never echoed. apiProperties.json declares connectionParameters.api_key (an apiKey securityDefinition alone does NOT create it — proven for cr1bd_ceparser). FOLDER creation + File-Request copy + shared-link mint + webhook lifecycle bind here; BYTES stay on first-party shared_box (CreateFile). Premium so it shares a DLP data group with the other custom service connectors. [DEPLOY-WITH-LOGIN] to import; bind the connection (Function key) is [RESERVED-FOR-USER]. The webhook RECEIVER route is NOT a connector op — Box posts to the Function directly (server-to-server).
Read-only 'List rows present in a table' over the job sheet. Drive/file ids are flow parameters. [RESERVED-FOR-USER] to run against the live SharePoint job sheet.

cedocumentmapper_v2 parser. Two ops on one Function/connection: ParseDocument (/parse, the 12-field EVA extraction, used by Flow_Parse) and ClassifyEmail (/classify-email, the deterministic Phase-8 inbound-email triage label, used by Flow_TriageClassify — ADR-0015). Function key (x-functions-key) stored on the connection, never in a definition. [DEPLOY-WITH-LOGIN] to import the connector (re-import to pick up the added /classify-email op).

connectionReferences.displayName: Collision Engineers OCR Host (custom: OcrPdf + PlateOcr)
connectionReferences.note: Phase-5 OCR fallback host (Azure Functions on Azure Container Apps). Two ops on one Function/connection: OcrPdf (/ocr-pdf, turns an image-only/scanned instruction PDF into text and — when the engine is present — the 12-field EVA extraction; used by Flow_Parse as the EMPTY-EXTRACTION fallback only) and PlateOcr (/plate-ocr, reads a registration plate from an overview photo for Evidence registrationVisible / VRM match; authored for a later phase, no flow binds it yet). Gating is enforced UPSTREAM in the flow/Code App (cr1bd_OCR_SCANNED_PDF_ENABLED / cr1bd_PLATE_OCR_ENABLED), NOT in the host. body/document + body/image are the RAW base64 strings (NOT @base64ToBinary) — the same Power Platform gateway double-encode landmine the parser hit; the host's tolerant decode peels the redundant layer (memory powerplatform-connector-base64-double-encode). Function key (x-functions-key) stored on the connection, never in a definition; the OCR host base is set at add-data-source/import time, never hardcoded. OCR_PROVIDER / PLATE_PROVIDER are CONTAINER-side settings, not Dataverse env-vars. Premium so it shares a DLP data group with the other custom service connectors. [DEPLOY-WITH-LOGIN] to import; [RESERVED-FOR-USER] to bind the connection (Function host key) + flip the gate at activation.

DVSA enrichment — direct Entra client_credentials to the DVSA MOT History API (+ DVLA). Secrets (DVSA/DVLA client id/secret/api-key) are Key Vault refs inside the Function, never in a definition. [DEPLOY-WITH-LOGIN] to import; secret value inject is [RESERVED-FOR-USER]. (ce-mcp-gateway retired — B1 obviated.)

M2.B (Path 1 of docs/plans/phase-1-intake-and-case-tracking/phase-1-operational.md §3.6, now DONE): status-evaluate.definition.json computes readiness via this connector's ValidateCase op, fronting the deployed functions/evavalidation Function — so the flow, the Code App computeReadiness() and the Function are ONE implementation of the EVA readiness contract (the Code App has a committed parity test against the same Function). Body-in { case, evidence }; returns { fieldsValid, imagesValid, openIssues[] }. The Function is pure domain logic ported from the canonical TS image-rules.ts + case-status.ts: gate-free (always on), holds NO secrets, makes NO upstream call — keyed by x-functions-key on the connection only. This repoint replaced the five inline Filter/Set actions that previously duplicated the contract. [DEPLOY-WITH-LOGIN] to import the connector; [RESERVED-FOR-USER] to bind the connection (Function host key) at activation.

Sentry REST v1.2. Token exchange + bearer live INSIDE the connector (Key Vault refs); the flow never reads EVA_CLIENT_SECRET. Only invoked when EVA_API_ENABLED=true. [RESERVED-FOR-USER] to enable/cutover.

notes: One connection reference per logical connector; flows bind to the reference, the reference binds to a connection at deploy/activation. This is what lets the SAME definition move dev -> test -> prod unchanged.

Premium connectors in play: Dataverse, Azure Blob, and the custom connectors (parser/DVSA/validation/EVA + the Phase-7 Box REST cr1bd_box_rest + the Phase-5 OCR host cr1bd_ocr) -> require Premium licensing (open Code Apps GA/licensing question). Standard: Office 365 Outlook, Excel Online (Business), Box first-party (the first-party Box connector is class Standard per Microsoft Learn — Standard + Premium connectors coexist in one DLP data group).

Phase-7 Box split (ADR-0012): TWO Box connections coexist by design. cr1bd_box_rest (Premium, custom, CCG via the box-webhook Function) does FOLDER create + File-Request copy + shared-link + webhook lifecycle; first-party cr1bd_box (Standard, interactive OAuth) is KEPT for finalize-eva-box's BYTE upload (CreateFile) only. This is the build-plan-04 PIN of the previously-unpinned connection-ref decision (parallel ref, NOT an in-place repoint).
DLP: every connector here must sit in the SAME data group in the target environment's DLP policy or import/run fails. Confirm before activation.
